title: elatostema repens var. viride
intro: Elatostema repens var. viride, also known as Green Creeping Vine or Green Elatostema, is a variety of the Elatostema repens plant, a type of flowering plant in the nettle family (Urticaceae) native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Elatostema repens var. viride include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional Asian medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
